records.specific_details: Outreach to relevant stakeholders to introduce Liquid Gas Ireland’s newly appointed Senior Director for Government Affairs and Policy, Alan Farrell, and highlight LGI’s policy priorities for rural consumers and businesses.
records.intended_results: To brief political representatives on LGI’s policy priorities to support the decarbonisation of rural homes and businesses beyond the natural gas grid, emphasising the need for a ‘Just Transition’ in Ireland and the potential impact of the upcoming Renewable Heat Obligation Bill.

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Update to members of Oireachtas on 221+ work
records.intended_results: Continued funding for 221+, Commitment & funding to extend services of 221+ to all women let down by the CervicalCheck screening programme, implement the AHR Act and push through Amendments bill, exclude 221+ member court payments from means testing, support gynaecological health, Implement Patient Voice Partner Policy, implement the 2018 Lymphoedema model of care, continued investment in Elimination of Cervical Cancer, Ringfenced funding for women’s health. Event sponsored by Marie Sherlock TD.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Lobbying by the Irish Lung Fibrosis Association for the establishment of a Clinical Care Programme for patients with lung fibrosis and the additional services needed.
records.intended_results: Support among members of Dáil and Seanad Éireann for the establishment of a Clinical Care Programme for patients with lung fibrosis in Ireland by the HSE, and for the additional services needed by those patients.

To share a copy of LauraLynn children hospices' statement welcoming the Programme for Government 2025 commitments to investing in palliative care to achieve full national coverage of hospice and community services; to developing a new national policy on palliative care for children and to increasing funding to children’s hospice care.2. To indicate LauraLynn's availability and willingness to practically support the progress of the Programme for Government commitments as appropriate.::To congratulate the new Minister for Health on her appointment
lobbying_activities: 1. To congratulate the new Minister for Health on her appointment.2. To share a copy of LauraLynn children hospices' statement welcoming the Programme for Government 2025 commitments.3. To indicate LauraLynn's availability and willingness to practically support the progress of the Programme for Government commitments as appropriate. - Letter

To contact government TDs who had signed a pledge to support LauraLynn to:1. To share a copy of LauraLynn children hospices' statement welcoming the Programme for Government 2025 commitments.2. To indicate LauraLynn's willingness to practically support the progress of the Programme for Government commitments as appropriate. - Email

records.specific_details: To establish an Oireachtas Heart and Stroke Group
records.intended_results: To encourage TDs and Senators from across the political spectrum to sign up to a group focusing on cardiovascular health::To increase awareness among TDs and Senators of important issues relating to cardiovascular health and achieve priority for the development of services and supports commensurate with the impact of cardiovascular disease in communities throughout Ireland
lobbying_activities: Phone calls with Deputy Cathal Crowe who hosted the AV room event and facilitated the event. - Phone call
Email

records.specific_details: Sought consultation on licensed firearms issues
records.intended_results: To seek establishment of a stakeholder forum for licensed firearms users
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: urge that a commitment be made to the development and delivery of a sustainable funding model for the local development sector. urge that a department dedicated to rural and community development, as DRCD is, remain in any new configuration under a new government.
records.intended_results: A sustainable model of funding for local development companies
lobbying_activities: Both the CEO and a board member contacted a number of Cllrs and TD to strongly urge that a commitment be made to the development and delivery of a sustainable funding model for the local development sector and that that a department dedicated to rural and community development, as DRCD is, remain in any new configuration under a new government. - Email

records.specific_details: GAEC-2 - IFA Spring Lobbying 2025
records.intended_results: To oppose the introduction of any mandatory restrictions on peat soils. The Ministers must defer any implementation of  Good Agricultural and Environmental Condition (GAEC -2) in 2025 and ensure that the upcoming EU Simplification Package no longer makes GAEC-2 a condition of receiving Basic Income Support for Sustainability (BISS) payments.

records.intended_results: See a change in the Legislation for Short Term rental providers in Ireland. Ref. STR legislation Bill  for STR.

Department of Housing, Local Government and Heritage

lobbying_activities: Email send by ISCF Members 'STR in Coastal & Rural Areas' - Email
Emailed the ISCF Programme for Government Proposal to specific government officials. Containing a proposal, context, solution and the ISCF's Role in this. - Email
Emails send with 'Solutions to the STR Issues' and 'Vision for STR' and 'An Analysis of the Self-catering Sector in Ireland' to Jackie Healy-Rea. - Email

ISCF proposals for inclusion in the Programme for Government.The future of rural tourism and in particular its’ main driver i.e. the short-term rental is under severe threat at present and the window for effective change is rather brief , hence this email to yourself. Asking for the ISCF proposals for inclusion in the Programme for Government, and protect local economies and communities to be incorporated in the Bill for STR. - Email

PQ on STTL - Received an email from Malcolm Byrne - Question to ask the Minister for Tourism on the 11/02/25; To ask the Minister for Tourism; Culture; Arts; Gaeltacht; Sport and Media for an update on the planned guidelines on short-term letting, on the introduction of a short-term letting register; his views on the importance of short-term letting for rural and coastal economic development; and if he will make a statement on the matter. - Email

Phone call made to Minister John Curran,  Norma Foley, Christopher O'Sullivan, Jackie Healy-Rea, discussing the STR Legislation and associated issues. Also received Phone calls from several TD's expressing their support. - Phone call
Press Release: ISCF Calls for “flawed” STL Bill to be “redrafted” - 19th Feb 2025. The full press release attached / https://iscf.ie/iscf-calls-for-flawed-stl-bill-to-be-redrafted/ - Email

Press Release: ISCF Urges Immediate Government Consultation on STR Regulations - 9th April 2025.The Irish Self-Catering Federation (ISCF) has said there is an “urgent' need for consultation with the industry as the government moves towards creating a register for all Short-Term-Rental properties by April of 2026. - Email

STTL Register / Meeting Request. Email send to Minister Peter Burke. Requesting a meeting to express our concerns over proposed legislation requiring all short-term rental properties in Ireland to obtain planning permission to operate legally. - Email

lobbying_activities: 1 meeting with Cllr Evie Sammon; 1 meeting with Cllr Bill Clear; 1 meeting with Deputy Verona Murphy. - Meeting
2 phone calls with Verona Murphy, 09.11.24 and 11.11.24. - Phone call

Email to election candidates, 12.11.24; emails to Barry Heneghan, 19.11.24 and 04.12.24; email to Tom Brabazon, 19.11.24; email to Cian O'Callaghan, 19.11.24; email to Naoise Ó'Muirí, 19.11.24; email to Jack Chambers, Roderic O'Gorman, Emer Currie, Paul Donnelly, Ruth Coppinger, 26/27.11.24; email to Brendan Smith, Pauline Tully, Niamh Smyth, Matt Carthy, 13.11.24; email to Eoin Hayes, Jim O'Callaghan, Chris Andrews, Emma Blain, Ivana Bacik, James Geoghegan, 01.11.24 - 30.11.24; - Email

Emails to Frank Feighan, Edel McSharry, Marian Harkin, 12.11.24 – 19.11.24; Email to Peter Cleere, Michael Doyle, Tom Kelly, 11.11.24, 12.11.24 & 15.11.24; Emails to Ger Carty, George Lawlor, Lisa McDonald, Frank Staples, Aoife Rose O’Brien, Verona Murphy, Michael MacDonncha, 11.11.24; emails to Michael Fitzmaurice, Claire Kerrane & Martin Daly. Email to Gillian Toole, 11.12.24; Email to Noel Grealish and Marian Harkin, 11.09.24; Emails to Deputy Michael Lowry’s office, 11.09.24 - 12.12.24. - Email

To attend a Clinical Trials Ireland event, 18.11.24; Emer Higgins, Shane Moynihan and Daithí Doolan in attendance. Asked for their support of the ICS election pledge. - Event / Reception
